subject: Artificial Intelligence
question: What are the basic components of an intelligent agent?
reason: An intelligent agent perceives the environment through sensors, acts through actuators, and maps percepts to actions through an agent function.

subject: Artificial Intelligence
question: Which of the following best describes Alpha-Beta pruning?

options.label: A
options.text: A method to eliminate branches that will not affect the final decision

options.label: B
options.text: A way to enhance stochastic sampling

options.label: C
options.text: A technique to always find deeper nodes

options.label: D
options.text: A replacement for the Minimax algorithm

correct_answer.label: A
correct_answer.text: A method to eliminate branches that will not affect the final decision

reason: Alpha-Beta pruning skips branches that cannot change the Minimax result, so the final decision remains the same with fewer explored nodes.

subject: Artificial Intelligence
question: What component of an agent is responsible for selecting actions?
reason: The agent function maps the percept sequence to an action, so it is responsible for choosing what the agent does.

subject: Artificial Intelligence
question: What is the evaluation function used in A* search?
reason: A* combines the actual path cost g(n) and the estimated remaining cost h(n) to select nodes.

subject: Artificial Intelligence
question: Which of the following is a key limitation of Greedy Best-First Search?

options.label: A
options.text: It is not guaranteed to find the optimal path

options.label: C
options.text: It uses both g(n) and h(n)

options.label: D
options.text: It has exponential time complexity in all cases

correct_answer.label: A
correct_answer.text: It is not guaranteed to find the optimal path

reason: Greedy Best-First Search uses the heuristic only, so it can choose a locally attractive path that is not globally optimal.

subject: Artificial Intelligence
question: Which of the following is a real-world use of production systems?
reason: Production systems use IF-THEN rules, which are common in rule-based expert systems such as medical diagnosis systems.

subject: Artificial Intelligence
question: What is the evaluation function used in Uniform Cost Search?

options.label: A
options.text: f (n) = g(n), where g(n) is the path cost from the start to node n

options.label: B
options.text: f (n) = h(n), heuristic value of n

correct_answer.label: A
correct_answer.text: f (n) = g(n), where g(n) is the path cost from the start to node n

reason: Uniform Cost Search expands the node with the lowest accumulated path cost, so only g(n) is used.

subject: Artificial Intelligence
question: Which of the following best represents: “Every student loves some course”?
reason: The statement means for each student there exists at least one course that the student loves.

subject: Artificial Intelligence
question: What is the primary goal of the Turing Test?

options.label: A
options.text: To evaluate whether a machine can exhibit intelligent behavior indistinguishable from a human

options.label: B
options.text: To measure the machine’s processing speed

options.label: C
options.text: To assess the logical reasoning ability of an AI system

options.label: D
options.text: To test the mathematical correctness of algorithms

correct_answer.label: A
correct_answer.text: To evaluate whether a machine can exhibit intelligent behavior indistinguishable from a human

reason: The Turing Test focuses on whether a machine’s conversational behavior appears human-like to an evaluator.

subject: Artificial Intelligence
question: Which component of the PEAS description ensures that an agent’s performance can be objectively evaluated regardless of its internal design?
reason: The performance measure defines the external criteria used to judge the success of the agent.

subject: Artificial Intelligence
question: Which of the following is a common failure scenario for basic hill climbing?

options.label: A
options.text: Getting stuck in a local maximum

correct_answer.label: A
correct_answer.text: Getting stuck in a local maximum

reason: Basic hill climbing can stop at a local maximum even when a better global solution exists.

subject: Artificial Intelligence
question: What differentiates informed search from uninformed search?

options.label: A
options.text: Use of heuristics to estimate goal distance

correct_answer.label: A
correct_answer.text: Use of heuristics to estimate goal distance

reason: Informed search uses heuristic knowledge to guide the search toward the goal.

subject: Artificial Intelligence
question: In adversarial search, which condition causes pruning to occur at a max node?

options.label: A
options.text: When the current node’s value is already greater than β

options.label: C
options.text: When max node equals min node

options.label: D
options.text: When the search depth is even

correct_answer.label: A
correct_answer.text: When the current node’s value is already greater than β

reason: At a max node, if the value is at least β, the minimizing parent will avoid that branch, so pruning can occur.

subject: Artificial Intelligence
question: Which one is not valid in first-order logic syntax?
reason: A quantified expression must contain a valid predicate or formula; ∀x(x) is not a well-formed predicate formula.

subject: Artificial Intelligence
question: When does A* behave exactly like Uniform Cost Search?

options.label: A
options.text: When the heuristic h(n) = 0 for all nodes

options.label: B
options.text: When the goal is a leaf node

options.label: C
options.text: When the graph has no branching

correct_answer.label: A
correct_answer.text: When the heuristic h(n) = 0 for all nodes

reason: If h(n) = 0, then A* uses f (n) = g(n), which is the same priority rule used by Uniform Cost Search. 1.2 Database and Data Mining

subject: Computer Network
question: What is the primary function of a protocol?

options.label: A
options.text: To define the format and order of messages exchanged between network entities

options.label: B
options.text: To define the physical characteristics of a network

options.label: C
options.text: To provide a user interface for network applications

options.label: D
options.text: To secure the network from all possible attacks

correct_answer.label: A
correct_answer.text: To define the format and order of messages exchanged between network entities

reason: A protocol specifies message formats, ordering, and actions taken on transmission or receipt.

subject: Computer Network
question: Which access network technology uses existing telephone lines?
reason: DSL provides broadband access over traditional telephone lines.

subject: Computer Network
question: In the five-layer Internet protocol stack, which layer is responsible for routing datagrams from source to destination?
reason: The network layer handles routing and forwarding of datagrams across networks.

subject: Computer Network
question: In the transport layer, what is network congestion?

options.label: A
options.text: A situation where too many sources send too much data too fast for the network to handle

options.label: B
options.text: A feature that guarantees packet delivery

options.label: C
options.text: The process of assigning port numbers to applications

options.label: D
options.text: A security protocol for encrypting data

correct_answer.label: A
correct_answer.text: A situation where too many sources send too much data too fast for the network to handle

reason: Congestion occurs when offered traffic exceeds network capacity, causing delay and loss.

subject: Computer Network
question: What is the primary purpose of the Domain Name System (DNS)?

options.label: A
options.text: To translate human-readable hostnames into IP addresses

options.label: B
options.text: To ensure the reliable delivery of data

options.label: C
options.text: To encrypt communication between a client and a server

options.label: D
options.text: To assign IP addresses to hosts

correct_answer.label: A
correct_answer.text: To translate human-readable hostnames into IP addresses

reason: DNS maps names such as domain names to numerical IP addresses.

subject: Computer Network
question: What is the difference between persistent and non-persistent HTTP?

options.label: A
options.text: Persistent HTTP can send multiple objects over a single TCP connection

options.label: B
options.text: Persistent HTTP is more secure than non-persistent HTTP

options.label: C
options.text: Non-persistent HTTP is faster than persistent HTTP

options.label: D
options.text: Non-persistent HTTP does not use TCP

correct_answer.label: A
correct_answer.text: Persistent HTTP can send multiple objects over a single TCP connection

reason: Persistent HTTP reuses the same TCP connection for multiple requests/responses.

subject: Computer Network
question: What is the primary protocol used for sending email between mail servers?
reason: SMTP is the standard protocol for transferring email between mail servers.

subject: Computer Network
question: What is the main idea behind Dynamic Adaptive Streaming over HTTP (DASH)?

options.label: A
options.text: To allow the client to choose the video quality based on available bandwidth

options.label: B
options.text: To stream video at a constant, high quality

options.label: C
options.text: To use a P2P network for video streaming

options.label: D
options.text: To eliminate the need for a client-side buffer

correct_answer.label: A
correct_answer.text: To allow the client to choose the video quality based on available bandwidth

reason: DASH lets the client adaptively request chunks at different bitrates according to bandwidth conditions.

subject: Computer Network
question: Which of the following is a key service provided by TCP but not by UDP?
reason: TCP provides reliability through acknowledgments, retransmission, and ordered delivery.

subject: Computer Network
question: What is the primary difference between Go-Back-N and Selective Repeat?

options.label: A
options.text: Selective Repeat can buffer out-of-order packets, while Go-Back-N cannot

options.label: B
options.text: Go-Back-N is more efficient than Selective Repeat

options.label: C
options.text: Go-Back-N uses a larger window size than Selective Repeat

options.label: D
options.text: Selective Repeat does not use ACKs

correct_answer.label: A
correct_answer.text: Selective Repeat can buffer out-of-order packets, while Go-Back-N cannot

reason: Selective Repeat accepts and buffers correct out-of-order packets; Go-Back-N discards them.

subject: Computer Network
question: What is TCP flow control?

options.label: A
options.text: A mechanism to prevent a sender from overwhelming a receiver

options.label: B
options.text: A mechanism to prevent a sender from overwhelming the network

options.label: C
options.text: A mechanism to ensure that packets arrive in the correct order

options.label: D
options.text: A mechanism to detect and retransmit lost packets

correct_answer.label: A
correct_answer.text: A mechanism to prevent a sender from overwhelming a receiver

reason: Flow control uses the receive window to match the sender’s rate to the receiver’s capacity.

subject: Computer Network
question: According to the provided slide, what does the network layer do to a transport-layer segment?

options.label: A
options.text: It encapsulates the segment with a network-layer header to create a datagram

options.label: B
options.text: It transfers the segment directly to the destination process

options.label: C
options.text: It adds a trailer for error checking

options.label: D
options.text: It breaks the segment into smaller pieces for transmission

correct_answer.label: A
correct_answer.text: It encapsulates the segment with a network-layer header to create a datagram

reason: The network layer wraps the transport segment inside a datagram by adding its own header.

subject: Computer Network
question: What is the difference between routing and forwarding?

options.label: A
options.text: Forwarding is a local function, while routing is a network-wide function

options.label: B
options.text: Routing is a local function, while forwarding is a network-wide function

options.label: C
options.text: Routing and forwarding are the same thing

options.label: D
options.text: Routing is done by hosts, while forwarding is done by routers

correct_answer.label: A
correct_answer.text: Forwarding is a local function, while routing is a networkwide function

reason: Forwarding moves packets at one router; routing computes paths across the network.

subject: Computer Network
question: What is the purpose of the Time-To-Live (TTL) field in the IPv4 header?

options.label: A
options.text: To limit the lifetime of a packet in the network

options.label: B
options.text: To indicate the time at which the packet was sent

options.label: C
options.text: To specify the size of the packet

options.label: D
options.text: To identify the source of the packet

correct_answer.label: A
correct_answer.text: To limit the lifetime of a packet in the network

reason: TTL is decremented at each router and prevents packets from circulating forever.

subject: Computer Network
question: What is a key advantage of IPv6 over IPv4?

options.label: A
options.text: It has a much larger address space

options.label: C
options.text: It is more secure by default

correct_answer.label: A

reason: IPv6 uses 128-bit addresses, giving a much larger address space than IPv4. 1.6 Operating System

subject: Algorithm Design

reason: A good algorithm should be unambiguous, so ambiguity is not a desirable characteristic.

subject: Algorithm Design
question: Consider the elements (6, 2, 11, 7, 5) sorted in ascending order using selection sort. What is the second smallest element in the list after the first swap?
reason: After the first selection-sort swap, 2 is placed first and the remaining order includes 5 as the next smallest listed value.

subject: Algorithm Design
question: In bubble sort, after the first complete pass through the array, which element is guaranteed to be in its correct final position?
reason: In ascending bubble sort, the largest element bubbles to the end after the first full pass.

subject: Algorithm Design
question: When does the recursion stop in a divide and conquer algorithm?

options.label: A
options.text: When an instance is too small to divide

options.label: C
options.text: When the problem is divided into two parts

options.label: D

correct_answer.label: A

subject: Algorithm Design
question: What is the key step repeated in Prim’s algorithm?

options.label: A

options.label: C
options.text: Removing the highest-weight edge from the graph

options.label: D
options.text: Sorting all edges by weight in each iteration

correct_answer.label: A
correct_answer.text: Adding the smallest edge connecting the MST to a new vertex

reason: Prim’s algorithm grows the MST by repeatedly selecting the minimum edge leaving the current tree.

subject: Algorithm Design
question: When sorting [6, 3, 8, 4] with Heap Sort, what happens immediately after building the max-heap [8, 6, 3, 4]?
reason: Heap sort moves the maximum root to the end by swapping it with the last heap element.

question: What is the key difference between the Fractional Knapsack and 0/1 Knapsack problems?

options.label: D
options.text: Fractional works only for integer weights

correct_answer.label: A

reason: Fractional knapsack permits taking parts of items, whereas 0/1 knapsack takes or leaves each item whole.
